title: New Relic .NET Agent Configuration
description: Fleet Control configuration schema for the New Relic .NET agent. Generated from src/Agent/NewRelic/Agent/Core/Config/Configuration.xsd. x-xml-attributes / x-xml-elements record the order required when rendering this config back into a newrelic.config XML file.

agentEnabled.description: Set this to true to enable the agent.

licenseKey.description: You must specify the license key associated with your New Relic account. This key binds your agent's data to your account in the New Relic service.

syncStartup.description: If true, the agent startup will block until a connection to the New Relic service is made.

autoStart.description: If false, the agent will not automatically start sending data to New Relic. You can use the Agent API to manually start the agent.

sendDataOnExit.description: If true, the agent will block process shutdown until it has gone through a harvest cycle and sent its data to the New Relic service.

sendDataOnExitThreshold.description: The amount of time (in milliseconds) an agent must run before sending data on process shutdown. The default is 60 seconds. This only applies when sendDataOnExit is set to true.

port.description: The proxy server port (optional - defaults to 8080).

user.description: The username used to authenticate with the proxy server (optional).

password.description: The password used to authenticate with the proxy server (optional).

passwordObfuscated.description: The obfuscated password with which to authenticate to the proxy server (optional).

domain.description: The domain used to authenticate with the proxy server (optional).

proxy.description: Proxy settings for connecting to the New Relic service. If a proxy is used, then the host attribute is required.

service.description: This section configures the agent to connect to the New Relic service.

disableSamplers.description: Samplers collect information about memory and CPU consumption. Set this to true to disable sampling.

name.description: If IIS is hosting multiple applications and you wish to have them report into New Relic as separate applications, create an application setting in the Web.Config of each application named NewRelic.AppName Set this to be the name of your application as you'd like it to show up in New Relic. New Relic will then auto-map instances of your application into an "application" on your home dashboard page. If youw ant to map this instance onto multiple applications, like "AJAX Requests" and "All UI", then specify up to three distinct names.

application.description: This element gives information about the application(s) being profiled.

level.description: Specify the agent's log level here. It may be one of (in any case): off, error, warn, info, debug, finest, all. To enable audit logging, set the auditLog attribute to true; don't set the log level to "audit".

enabled.description: If this is false, no attempts will be made to write to a log file. This is primarily used for installations on read-only file systems.

directory.description: The directory to hold log files generated by the agent. If this is omitted, then a directory named 'logs' in the New Relic Agent installation area will be used by default.

fileName.description: The name of the log file to write. If this is omitted, then the name is derived from the name of the monitored process.

console.description: If this is true, then log messages are also appended to the console, in addition to going to the log file.

auditLog.description: If this is true, then any data that is sent to New Relic will be recorded in a separate log file, in addition to going to the normal log file. The audit log file will be named the same as the normal log file except that it will have "_audit" appended to the file name (for example, "MyApp_audit.log").

logRollingStrategy.description: The strategy to use for rolling over the Agent log file. Default is size.

maxLogFileSizeMB.description: The maximum size of the Agent log file in megabytes before it is rolled over. Default is 50MB. If this is set to 0, no size limit will be enforced. Only used if logRollingStrategy is set to "size".

maxLogFiles.description: The maximum number of Agent log files to keep. Defaults to 4. If this is set to 0, then all log files will be kept.

log.description: The agent generates its own log file to keep its logging information separate from that of your application.

enabled.description: Turns on capturing. Enabled by default.

allowAllHeaders.description: Allow all request headers to be captured as attributes.

enabled.description: Turns on attribute capturing. Enabled by default.

include.description: The name of a specific custom attribute to include.

exclude.description: The name of a specific custom attribute to exclude.

attributes.description: The attributes element tells the transaction tracer, error collector, browser monitoring and transaction events whether or not to capture any attributes.

enabled.description: Disabled: Turns the event recorder on or off. Defaults to true.

maximumSamplesPerMinute.description: Disabled: The maximum number of samples to send per minute. It is possible that fewer events will be sent, but never more. Default is 10000.

maximumSamplesStored.description: Disabled: The maximum number of samples to store in memory at a time. Default is 10000.

captureAttributes.description: Disabled: Include custom parameters in transaction analytics events. Defaults to true.

enabled.description: Disabled: Turns event recording of transaction events on or off. Defaults to true.

analyticsEvents.description: Disabled: Collects semi-detailed information about various events that occur in an application.

enabled.description: Turns the custom event recorder on or off. Default is true. Note: The agent will store up to 10,000 custom events in memory at a time.

maximumSamplesStored.description: The maximum number of samples to store in memory at a time. Default is 10000.

enabled.description: Turns on custom attribute capturing for Custom Events

include.description: The name of a specific custom attribute to include in the custom events.

exclude.description: The name of a specific custom attribute to exclude in the custom events.

customEvents.description: Collects information about custom events that occur in an application.

enabled.description: Turns the event recorder on or off. Default is true.

maximumSamplesPerMinute.description: Disabled: The maximum number of samples to send per minute. It is possible that fewer events will be sent, but never more. Default is 10000.

maximumSamplesStored.description: The maximum number of samples to store in memory at a time. Default is 10000.

enabled.description: Turns event recording of transaction events on or off. Default is true.
Turns on custom attribute capturing for Transaction Events

include.description: The name of a specific custom attribute to include in the transaction events.

exclude.description: The name of a specific custom attribute to exclude in the transaction events.

transactionEvents.description: Collects semi-detailed information about various events that occur in an application.

putForDataSend.description: POST is the default for data transmission. This setting, when true, enforces PUT for data transmission.

compressedContentEncoding.description: Controls the encoding for compressed content. deflate is the default.

dataTransmission.description: These settings control how data is sent to New Relic.

transactionThreshold.description: The threshold (in milliseconds) for when to collect a transaction trace. When the response time of a controller action exceeds this threshold, a transaction trace will be recorded and sent to New Relic. Valid values are any float value, or (default) "apdex_f", which will use the threshold for an dissatisfying Apdex controller action - four times the Apdex T value.

recordSql.description: This describes how to record the SQL captured for the slow SQL monitor.

explainEnabled.description: This determines whether the agent will capture query plans for slow SQL queries. Only supported in SQL Server, MySql, and postgres. Should be set to false when using other adapters.

explainThreshold.description: The threshold for query execution time (in milliseconds) below which query plans will not be captured. Relevant only when 'explainEnabled' is true.

maxSegments.description: The maximum number of segments to collect in a transaction trace.

maxStackTrace.description: The maximum number of stack traces to collect during a harvest cycle.

maxExplainPlans.description: The maximum number of explain plans to collect during a harvest cycle.

captureAttributes.description: Disabled: Include user custom parameters in transaction traces.

enabled.description: Turns on custom attribute capturing for Transaction Traces

include.description: The name of a specific custom attribute to include in the transaction traces.

exclude.description: The name of a specific custom attribute to exclude in the transaction traces.

transactionTracer.description: The transaction tracer captures deep information about slow transactions and sends this to the New Relic service once a minute. Included in the transaction is the exact call sequence of the transactions including any SQL statements issued.

enabled.description: This setting controls whether or not the host and the port, path, or ID are reported for datastores.
This setting controls whether or not database names are reported for datastores.

WARNING: Enabling this setting has the potential of collecting sensitive data or personally identifiable information within your queries. This setting controls whether or not database query parameters are reported for slow SQL queries. The transactionTracer.recordSql setting must be set to 'raw'. This setting is ignored when configurable security policies have been applied or High Security Mode is enabled.

datastoreTracer.description: These settings control what type of database information is sent to New Relic.

enabled.description: It is enabled by default. Set this to false to turn it off.

compression.description: Set this to false to disable compression of data being transmitted to the trace observer. Compression is enabled by default.

host.description: The host name of the infinite tracing observer to which the agent should send spans. This host name should not include protocol (such as http: or https:) and should not include a port

port.description: The port on the trace observer to send spans to.

enabled.description: It is disabled by default. Set this to true to turn it on.

excludeNewrelicHeader.description: Set this to true to exclude the newrelic header from outgoing requests. It is included by default.

enableSuccessMetrics.description: Set this to false to disable the payload accept/create success supportability metrics. It is enabled by default.

account_id.description: The account id to use for distributed tracing. Required in serverless mode if distributed tracing is enabled.

trusted_account_key.description: The trusted account key to use for distributed tracing. Required in serverless mode if distributed tracing is enabled.

primary_application_id.description: The optional primary application id to use for distributed tracing. Used only in serverless mode.

root.description: Controls span sampling at the root of the transaction.

remoteParentSampled.description: Controls span sampling behavior when the trace parent was sampled.

remoteParentNotSampled.description: Controls span sampling behavior when the trace parent was not sampled.

distributedTracing.description: Distributed tracing lets you see the path that a request takes through your distributed system. Enabling distributed tracing changes the behavior of some New Relic features, so carefully consult the transition guide before you enable this feature: https://docs.newrelic.com/docs/transition-guide-distributed-tracing Default is false.

enabled.description: Enables the collection of span events. Defaults to true.

maximumSamplesStored.description: The maximum number of samples to store in memory at a time. Default is 2000.

enabled.description: Enable or disable attribute capturing for span events. Default is true. If attributes.enabled at the root level is false, no attributes will be sent on span events regardless on how this property is set.

include.description: The name of an attribute to include in span events.

exclude.description: The name of an attribute to exclude in span events.

spanEvents.description: Configuration controlling what information is collected on spans as part of a distributed trace.

enabled.description: It is disabled by default.
It is disabled by default.

stripExceptionMessages.description: Enables/Disables the stripping of error messages for errors sent to APM.

enabled.description: The slowSQL service is enabled by default.

slowSql.description: The slowSql service captures information about slow SQL executions, and captures and obfuscates explain plans for these queries.

enabled.description: The error collector is enabled by default. Set this to false to turn it off. This feature is only available in the Professional product edition.

captureAttributes.description: Disabled: Include user custom parameters in traced errors. Defaults to true.

maxEventSamplesStored.description: Reservoir limit for error events. Defaults to 100.

exception.description: Disabled: The full name of an exception to ignore, such as "System.IO.FileNotFoundException".

ignoreErrors.description: Disabled: To stop specific exceptions from reporting to New Relic, list them here. Use the full name of the exception, such as "System.IO.FileNotFoundException".

code.description: You may use standard integral HTTP errorcodes, such as just 401, or may use Microsoft FullStatusCodes, with decimal points, such as 401.4 or 403.18

ignoreStatusCodes.description: To stop specific error codes from reporting to New Relic, list them here.

expectedStatusCodes.description: A comma separated list of status codes to be marked as expected. You may use standard integral HTTP errorcodes, such as just 401, or may use Microsoft FullStatusCodes, with decimal points, such as 401.4 or 403.18.

enabled.description: Turns on custom attribute capturing for the Error Collector.

include.description: The name of a specific custom attribute to include in the error trace.

exclude.description: The name of a specific custom attribute to exclude in the error trace.

errorCollector.description: The error collector captures information about uncaught exceptions and sends them to the New Relic service for viewing.

autoInstrument.description: By default the agent automatically injects the JavaScript for RUM into ASP pages. Set this attribute to false to turn off this behavior.

loader.description: This attribute defines the type of Javascript functionality to be injected. By default just RUM instrumentation is injected. Other valid values are "full" for Javascript Errors and possibly future features, and "none" which means no javascript injection.

sslForHttp.description: This attribute specifies whether the code injected into pages will communicate with New Relic servers using SSL even when the page original request does not.

captureAttributes.description: Disabled: Include user custom parameters in the RUM client configuration. Defaults to false.

regex.description: The regular expression to be evaluated.

items.description: Each path entry is a regular expression to be evaluated by the .NET Framework's regular expression engine. These regular expressions should be easy and quick to evaluate, otherwise they can adversely affect application performance.

path.description: Each path entry is a regular expression to be evaluated by the .NET Framework's regular expression engine. These regular expressions should be easy and quick to evaluate, otherwise they can adversely affect application performance.

requestPathsExcluded.description: When Browser Monitoring is enabled for auto-instrumentation, this list of request paths will be used to exclude pages from being instrumented for browser monitoring.

enabled.description: Turns on custom attribute capturing for Browser Monitoring

include.description: The name of a specific custom attribute to include in browser monitoring.

exclude.description: The name of a specific custom attribute to exclude in browser monitoring.

browserMonitoring.description: The New Relic Real User Monitoring (RUM) gives you insight into the performance real users are experiencing with your website. This is accomplished by measuring the time it takes for your users' browsers to download and render your web pages by injecting a small amount of JavaScript code into the header and footer of each page.

enabled.description: Controls whether or not AI monitoring features are enabled. When set to 'false', all AI monitoring sub-features are disabled. Defaults to false.
Controls whether or not AI monitoring of streams is enabled. Defaults to true.

streaming.description: When enabled, AI monitoring of streams occurs.

enabled.description: Controls whether or not AI monitoring will include the content of the chat and embedding messages. Defaults to true.

recordContent.description: When enabled, AI monitoring events will include the content of the chat and embedding messages.

aiMonitoring.description: Allows configuration of AI monitoring instrumentation.

enabled.description: Controls whether any logging features are enabled. When set to 'false', all logging sub-features are disabled. Defaults to 'true'.
Controls whether or not logging metrics are captured. Defaults to true.

metrics.description: When enabled, sends up metrics on the number of log lines being created in total and at different severity levels.

enabled.description: Controls whether or not log forwarding occurs. Defaults to false.

maxSamplesStored.description: The maximum number of log messages captured per minute. Default is 10000 messages.

logLevelDenyList.description: A comma-separated, case-insensitive, list of log level names from the selected logging framework that should be ignored and not sent up to New Relic.

enabled.description: Controls whether or not context attributes filtering is used. Defaults to false.

include.description: A comma-separated list of case-sensitive strings that define the context attributes that should be added to the log record. Empty means include all.

exclude.description: A comma-separated list of case-sensitive strings that define the context attributes that should NOT be added to the log record. "exclude" wins over "include".

enabled.description: Controls whether or not labels are included with log records. Defaults to false.

exclude.description: A comma-separated list of case-insensitive strings that define the labels that should NOT be added to log records.

labels.description: Include configured labels with log records.

forwarding.description: When enabled, capture and send up log messages with logs-in-context data.

enabled.description: Controls whether or not local log messages are decorated with logs-in-context data. Defaults to false.

localDecorating.description: When enabled, local log messages will be decorated with logs-in-context data as they are written. The method used to decorate the messages depends on the logging framework and may require additional configuration.

applicationLogging.description: Allows configuration of application logging instrumentation.

labels.description: Labels allow you to tag your applications so you can filter them in APM.

detectAws.description: This attribute controls whether to poll the AWS metadata endpoint (optional). The default is true.

detectAzure.description: This attribute controls whether to poll the Azure metadata endpoint (optional). The default is true.

detectGcp.description: This attribute controls whether to poll the Google Cloud Platform metadata endpoint (optional). The default is true.

detectPcf.description: This attribute controls whether to look for Pivotal Cloud Foundry environment variables (optional). The default is true.

detectDocker.description: This attribute controls whether to attempt reading the proc/self/cgroup file for Docker (optional). The default is true.

detectKubernetes.description: This attribute controls whether to look for Kubernetes environment variables (optional). The default is true.

detectAzureFunction.description: This attribute controls whether to look for Azure Function environment variables (optional). The default is true.

detectAzureAppService.description: This attribute controls whether to look for Azure AppService environment variables (optional). The default is true.

logicalProcessors.description: The value of this attribute is the total number of hyper-threaded execution contexts available (optional).

totalRamMib.description: The value of this attribute represents the total amount of memory available to the host, not the process, in mebibytes (1024 squared or 1,048,576 bytes) (optional).

billingHost.description: The value of this attribute is the server name you have configured (optional). This may be differnet from the actual server hostname.

utilization.description: Elements under utilization allow you to configure the gathering of utilization and pricing information by agents during connect.

enabled.description: Set this to "false" to disable capturing namespace and function information on spans for code level metrics in CodeStream.

codeLevelMetrics.description: Turns on/off capture of code level metric attributes.

accountId.description: The AWS Account ID used by this application.

cloud.description: Provide linking data needed for certain cloud services.

enabled.description: Global setting for OpenTelemetry signal processing in the agent. If set to "false", no OpenTelemetry signals will be processed or sent to New Relic. If set to "true", individual signal types (e.g., metrics) can be controlled via their specific enabled settings. Environment variable: NEW_RELIC_OPENTELEMETRY_ENABLED

This setting controls whether OpenTelemetry tracing is enabled. Defaults to "true". This feature must be used together with the global openTelemetry enabled setting. Environment variable: NEW_RELIC_OPENTELEMETRY_TRACES_ENABLED

include.description: A comma-delimited list of OpenTelemetry Tracers (Activity Source Names) to include for tracing. This setting can override the built-in exclusion list, but can be overridden by the user-configured 'exclude' list (exclude has highest precedence). Environment variable: NEW_RELIC_OPENTELEMETRY_TRACES_INCLUDE

exclude.description: A comma-delimited list of OpenTelemetry Tracers (Activity Source Names) to exclude from tracing. Tracers matching these names will not have their traces exported. This has the highest precedence and can override items in the include list. Environment variable: NEW_RELIC_OPENTELEMETRY_TRACES_EXCLUDE

enabled.description: This setting controls whether OpenTelemetry metrics collection and export to New Relic is enabled. Defaults to "true". This feature must be used together with the global openTelemetry enabled setting. Environment variable: NEW_RELIC_OPENTELEMETRY_METRICS_ENABLED

include.description: A comma-delimited list of OpenTelemetry Meter names to include for metrics export to New Relic. Meters defined in this list will be enabled This setting can override the built-in exclusion list, but can be overridden by the user-configured 'exclude' list (exclude has highest precedence). Environment variable: NEW_RELIC_OPENTELEMETRY_METRICS_INCLUDE

exclude.description: A comma-delimited list of OpenTelemetry Meter names to exclude from metrics export to New Relic. Meters matching these names will not have their metrics exported. This has the highest precedence and can override items in the include list. Environment variable: NEW_RELIC_OPENTELEMETRY_METRICS_EXCLUDE

export_interval.description: The number of milliseconds between each attempt to ship opentelemetry metrics. This value must be equal to or greater than the value of export_timeout. If export_interval is less than export_timeout, the agent will log a warning and use the default values for both settings. Default: 60000 (60 seconds) Environment variable: NEW_RELIC_OPENTELEMETRY_METRICS_EXPORT_INTERVAL

export_timeout.description: The number of milliseconds an export operation is allowed in order to successfully complete. This value must be equal to or less than the value of export_interval. Default: 10000 (10 seconds) Environment variable: NEW_RELIC_OPENTELEMETRY_METRICS_EXPORT_TIMEOUT

metrics.description: Configuration for OpenTelemetry metrics collection and export.

openTelemetry.description: OpenTelemetry configuration for the agent. This section contains configuration options that control OpenTelemetry signal processing and export to New Relic.
